questions.questionText: A, B and C are points on a straight line. Angle x is adjacent to an angle of 110°. What is the size of angle x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So, x = 180° - 110° = 70°.

questions.questionText: Three angles are around a point. Two of the angles are 150° and 90°. What is the size of the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. The third angle is 360° - 150° - 90° = 120°.

questions.questionText: Two straight lines intersect. One of the angles formed is 55°. What is the size of the vertically opposite angle?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal. Therefore, the angle is 55°.

questions.questionText: In a triangle, two of the angles are 60° and 70°. What is the size of the third angle?
questions.explanation: The angles in a triangle add up to 180°. The third angle is 180° - 60° - 70° = 50°.

questions.questionText: In the diagram, line L1 is parallel to line L2. What is the value of angle a?
questions.explanation: Angle 'a' and the angle marked 48° are alternate interior angles. Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel. So, a = 48°.

questions.questionText: In the diagram, line M1 is parallel to line M2. What is the value of angle b?
questions.explanation: Angle 'b' and the angle marked 118° are corresponding angles. Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel. So, b = 118°.

questions.questionText: In the diagram, line P1 is parallel to line P2. An interior angle on the same side of the transversal is 105°. What is the value of angle c?
questions.explanation: Angle 'c' and an angle of 105° are allied (or co-interior) angles. Allied angles add up to 180°. So, c = 180° - 105° = 75°.

questions.questionText: A triangle is isosceles. One of its base angles is 40°. What is the size of the angle at the vertex?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal, so both are 40°. The angles in a triangle add up to 180°. The vertex angle is 180° - 40° - 40° = 100°.

questions.questionText: Find the size of the exterior angle, x, of the triangle shown.
questions.explanation: The exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the two opposite interior angles. So, x = 50° + 60° = 110°.

questions.questionText: A quadrilateral has angles 100°, 80°, and 130°. What is the size of the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: The interior angles of a quadrilateral add up to 360°. The fourth angle is 360° - (100° + 80° + 130°) = 360° - 310° = 50°.

questions.questionText: Consider two intersecting straight lines. If one of the angles formed is 70°, what is the reason that the vertically opposite angle is also 70°?
options: Angles on a straight line add to 180°
Angles around a point add to 360°
questions.explanation: When two straight lines cross, the angles opposite each other are equal. This property is known as 'vertically opposite angles are equal'.

questions.questionText: An equilateral triangle has three equal sides and three equal angles. What is the size of each angle?
questions.explanation: The angles in a triangle add up to 180°. In an equilateral triangle, all three angles are equal. Therefore, each angle is 180° / 3 = 60°.
